title: Property Management Operations Score
subtitle: Find the exact workflows where your team is losing hours every week

persona_selector.text: How many units does your company currently manage?

answers.label: Under 250 units
250 to 750 units
750 to 2,000 units
Over 2,000 units

questions.text: When a tenant submits a request, how quickly does it get a first response?

answers.label: Often takes more than 24 hours, sometimes longer
Within 12 to 24 hours on most days
Within a few hours during business hours
Within 30 minutes through a shared inbox or tool
Instant auto-acknowledgment with routing and ticket ID

questions.text: How are inbound tenant messages across channels (email, phone, text, portal) consolidated?

answers.label: Each channel is separate, nothing is unified
One person manually checks each channel
Email and portal are unified, phone and text are separate
Most channels feed one shared inbox
All channels unified with automated tagging and routing

questions.text: How often do tenants have to repeat the same information to different team members?

answers.label: Almost always, there is no shared context
Frequently, notes live in different places
Sometimes, depending on who picks up the thread
Rarely, we keep a shared record per tenant
Never, every team member sees full history instantly

questions.text: How are repetitive tenant questions (rent, policies, amenities) handled?

answers.label: Every question gets a full manual reply
Team members copy-paste from personal notes
We have a shared doc of templates
Canned responses inside our tool, manually triggered
AI assistant handles FAQs and escalates only edge cases

low.headline: Your inbox is running your team
low.text: Inbound requests are likely eating 15 or more hours a week across your office. Tenants repeat themselves, messages get lost, and response times slip. This is the first place to fix because the leverage is enormous.
steps: Consolidate email, portal, text, and voicemail into one shared inbox this week
Build a top 20 FAQ template library and require the team to use it
Add an auto-acknowledgment so every tenant gets an instant reply with a ticket ID

mid.headline: You have structure, now add intelligence
mid.text: You have a shared system but your team still does the routing and triage work by hand. A thin layer of automation on top of what you already have will recover 8 to 12 hours a week.
steps: Add automatic tagging by request type (maintenance, billing, lease, general)
Route urgent issues to a priority queue with SLA tracking
Deploy an AI assistant that drafts replies to the top 15 recurring questions

high.headline: Communication is a strength, defend it
high.text: Tenant comms are running clean. The risk now is that growth breaks the system you built. Add measurement and guardrails before volume doubles.
steps: Track first-response time and resolution time per category weekly
Add sentiment detection to flag frustrated tenants before they churn
Review AI assistant accuracy monthly and expand coverage to secondary topics

questions.text: How are new leases generated and sent for signature?

answers.label: Manual Word docs emailed back and forth
PDF templates, signed by hand or scanned
Standard template with e-signature tool
Templated, pre-filled from tenant data, e-signed
Fully automated from application approval to signed lease

questions.text: How do renewals get triggered and managed?

answers.label: Someone remembers and sends emails manually
Calendar reminders at 60 and 30 days out
Spreadsheet tracks expirations, manual outreach
Automated renewal reminders sent to tenants
End-to-end automated renewal workflow with pricing logic

questions.text: Where are signed leases and related documents stored?

answers.label: Mix of email attachments, local drives, and paper files
Shared drive, inconsistent folder structure
Shared drive with a naming convention
Property management software with document module
Central system with tags, search, and access controls

questions.text: How do you track compliance items like insurance, inspections, and certifications?

answers.label: We find out when something expires or breaks
Manual spreadsheet, checked occasionally
Spreadsheet with reminder dates
Automated reminders to tenants and vendors
Full dashboard with auto-escalation when items lapse

low.headline: Lease ops are costing you rent days
low.text: Every slow renewal and manually routed lease is a direct hit to cash flow and tenant experience. This is the area where automation pays for itself inside one quarter.
steps: Move all leases into one e-signature platform with pre-filled templates
Build a single renewal tracker and set automatic 90, 60, and 30 day alerts
Centralize all signed documents in one searchable location this month

mid.headline: Tighten the renewal and compliance loops
mid.text: Your documents are in order but renewals and compliance still lean on human memory. Closing those gaps protects revenue and removes a constant source of small fires.
steps: Automate renewal outreach with dynamic pricing pulled from market data
Build a compliance dashboard showing every expiring item across the portfolio
Connect lease signing to your property management system so data flows automatically

high.headline: Lease operations are a competitive edge
high.text: Your document workflows are tight. Use that foundation to unlock better tenant experience and faster reporting for owners.
steps: Offer owners a live dashboard of lease status, renewals, and compliance
Integrate lease data with financial forecasting for occupancy projections
Add AI review of incoming lease amendments and addenda for risk flags

questions.text: How are maintenance requests triaged and assigned to vendors?

answers.label: Office manager reads every request and calls vendors
Team uses a shared spreadsheet to track assignments
Work order software, manually assigned
Rules-based auto-routing by category and property
AI triage with priority, vendor match, and cost estimate

questions.text: How do you follow up with vendors on open work orders?

answers.label: Phone calls and memory
Someone checks a list every few days
Daily stand-up or status email
Automated vendor reminders when SLAs slip
Live dashboard with SLA tracking and auto-escalation

questions.text: How are tenants kept informed about the status of their requests?

answers.label: Only when they follow up with us
Manual update emails when we remember
Team sends updates at key milestones
Automated status updates at each stage
Self-service portal with real-time status and photos

questions.text: How do you capture and analyze maintenance cost and recurrence data?

answers.label: We do not track this in any real way
Invoices live in accounting, no cross-analysis
Quarterly manual review of spend by property
Monthly reports by category and vendor
Live dashboards showing cost per unit, recurrence, and vendor performance

low.headline: Maintenance is the biggest hours drain in your business
low.text: Routing, following up, and updating tenants by hand is likely eating 20+ hours a week. Fixing even half of this recovers a headcount of capacity.
steps: Move every work order into one system, no more phone-and-paper requests
Create a vendor matrix mapping request types to preferred vendors
Turn on automatic tenant status updates at submission, assignment, and completion

mid.headline: Your work order system needs a brain
mid.text: You have the pipes, now add logic. Rules and automation on top of your current tool can cut manual touches per work order in half.
steps: Build auto-routing rules by category, property, and vendor capacity
Set SLA timers with automatic reminders and escalation
Launch a tenant-facing status view so they stop calling for updates

high.headline: Turn maintenance data into a profit lever
high.text: Your operations are clean. Now use the data to negotiate better vendor rates, predict issues, and show owners real value.
steps: Run monthly vendor scorecards and renegotiate the bottom performers
Add predictive flags for recurring issues at the unit and property level
Build an owner-facing maintenance report that auto-generates each month

questions.text: How is rent collection handled and tracked?

answers.label: Mix of checks, transfers, and manual recording
Online portal for most tenants, manual follow-up
Portal with automated reminders for late payments
Portal plus automated late fees and payment plans
Full automation including delinquency workflows and reporting

questions.text: How are owner statements and reports produced?

answers.label: Manual, built in spreadsheets each month
Template in accounting software, edited each time
Standard report from our PM software
Automated monthly reports sent on schedule
Live owner portal with 24/7 access to financials

questions.text: How do you handle owner questions and ad-hoc reporting requests?

answers.label: Team pulls numbers manually each time
We have a few saved reports we modify
Most questions answered from a shared dashboard
Owners can self-serve most answers in a portal
AI assistant answers owner questions from live financial data

questions.text: How are invoices, bills, and expenses processed?

answers.label: Paper invoices, manual data entry
Email invoices, manually entered into accounting
Upload and match inside accounting software
OCR capture with approval workflows
Fully automated invoice capture, coding, approval, and payment

low.headline: Financial ops are silently burning cash
low.text: Manual bookkeeping, late payments, and hand-built owner reports cost you both hours and trust. Owners quietly churn when they feel in the dark.
steps: Require online rent payments for all tenants with automatic reminders
Standardize owner reports into one monthly template sent the same day each month
Adopt an OCR bill-capture tool to stop manual invoice entry this quarter

mid.headline: Give owners the transparency they want
mid.text: Your books are in decent shape but owners still rely on you for answers. A self-service layer builds trust and frees your team from recurring questions.
steps: Launch an owner portal with live statements and property performance
Automate monthly reporting delivery with commentary on key variances
Set up approval workflows so bills move without constant back-and-forth

high.headline: Financial ops are a growth engine
high.text: Your operations are strong. Now use automation to scale without adding accounting headcount and turn reporting into a sales asset.
steps: Offer predictive cash flow and expense forecasting to owners
Connect AI assistant to financial data so owners get instant answers
Package your reporting quality into your sales pitch for new owner acquisition

questions.text: How many separate tools does your team log into each day?

answers.label: More than 10, with a lot of copy-paste between them
Seven to 10, mostly disconnected
Five or six, some integrated
Three or four core tools, well integrated
Unified platform with all data flowing into one system

questions.text: When a new team member joins, how do they learn your processes?

answers.label: They shadow someone and pick it up by osmosis
A few Word docs exist but are out of date
We have a shared drive of SOPs
Structured onboarding with checklists and videos
Living knowledge base with searchable SOPs and role playbooks

questions.text: How do tasks and handoffs get tracked across your team?

answers.label: Email, texts, and sticky notes
A shared spreadsheet or calendar
Task tool, but adoption is inconsistent
Everyone works out of one project tool daily
Automated task creation from triggers (requests, leases, inspections)

questions.text: How confident are you that your reporting data is accurate across tools?

answers.label: Not confident, numbers rarely match between systems
We spend time each month reconciling discrepancies
Mostly accurate, with known gaps
Accurate thanks to integrations and regular audits
Single source of truth with real-time syncing across tools

low.headline: Your tool stack is fighting your team
low.text: Disconnected tools and tribal knowledge are the slow leak under everything else. Until this is fixed, every other improvement gets diluted.
steps: Map every tool your team uses and identify the top three copy-paste pain points
Choose one project tool and require the whole team to live inside it
Start an SOP library this week, one process documented per day

mid.headline: Connect the pieces you already have
mid.text: You have good bones but the tools are not talking. Integrations and better data hygiene will remove most of the friction without a platform change.
steps: Build two to three integrations connecting your top tools
Run a quarterly tool audit, kill anything under 50 percent adoption
Assign an ops lead to own the SOP library and keep it current

high.headline: Scale without adding headcount
high.text: Your systems are dialed in. The next move is using that foundation to grow without proportional hiring.
steps: Invest in automation and AI agents that handle repeatable work end to end
Build internal dashboards tracking ops KPIs the executive team reviews weekly
Use your clean data to pilot predictive analytics for retention and maintenance

cta.headline: Turn your score into a 90-day action plan
cta.description: Book a free 30-minute strategy call and I will walk you through your results, identify the single biggest time-recovery opportunity for your portfolio size, and show you exactly what a custom automation build would look like for your company.
